questions.question: The area of a rectangle is 240 cm² and its length is 20 cm. Find its width.
A square has area 256 cm². Find its perimeter.
Find the base of a triangle whose area is 84 cm² and height is 7 cm.
A parallelogram has area 180 cm² and height 12 cm. Find its base.
Find the diagonals of a rhombus if its area is 96 cm² and one diagonal is 12 cm.
Find the height of a trapezium with area 120 cm² and parallel sides 10 cm and 14 cm.
The base of a triangle is doubled while its height remains the same. What happens to its area?
If both length and width of a rectangle are doubled, what happens to the area?
Find the area of a triangle whose base is 30 cm and height is 16 cm.
Find the area of a parallelogram with base 18 cm and height 14 cm.
Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 32 cm and 18 cm.
Find the area of a trapezium whose parallel sides are 30 cm and 18 cm and height is 10 cm.
A square and a rectangle have the same area. If the square has side 12 cm and the rectangle length is 18 cm, find the width of the rectangle.
Find the area of a triangle whose base is 25 cm and height is 20 cm.
Find the area of a parallelogram whose base is 22 cm and height is 15 cm.
Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 40 cm and 16 cm.
Find the area of a trapezium with parallel sides 35 cm and 15 cm and height 12 cm.
Explain how a parallelogram can be transformed into a rectangle of the same area.
questions.answer: By cutting a triangular portion from one side and placing it on the other side to form a rectangle with the same base and height.

questions.question: Explain how two identical triangles can form a parallelogram.
questions.answer: When two congruent triangles are joined along their corresponding sides, they form a parallelogram.

questions.question: Find the area of a triangle whose base is 40 cm and height is 12 cm.
Find the area of a parallelogram whose base is 28 cm and height is 11 cm.
Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 36 cm and 20 cm.
Find the area of a trapezium with parallel sides 40 cm and 24 cm and height 15 cm.
Explain how an irregular polygon can be used to calculate area.
questions.answer: An irregular polygon can be divided into triangles or rectangles and their areas added to obtain the total area.

questions.question: Why are square units used to measure area?
questions.answer: Because area measures two-dimensional surface coverage, which is represented using square units.
